items.title: Rybakina wins maiden Australian Open by defeating Sabalenka
items.summary: Elena Rybakina defeated world No 1 Aryna Sabalenka 6-4 4-6 6-4 to win her second Grand Slam title with a maiden success at the Australian Open.

items.title: AC Milan lijkt PSV af te troeven in strijd om Cissè
items.summary: Alphadjo Cissè gaat hoogstwaarschijnlijk niet voor PSV, maar voor AC Milan kiezen. Tot voor kort dacht de koploper van de Eredivisie de handtekening van de 19-jarige aanvallende middenvelder binnen te gaan halen, totdat I Rossoneri de deal kaapten.

items.title: Het lichtpuntje in het rampseizoen van Wolverhampton Wanderers
items.summary: In de rubriek VI Scout iedere week aandacht voor talenten die indruk maken op de internationale velden. In deze aflevering Mateus Mané van Wolverhampton Wanderers.

items.title: Moder eindelijk terug bij Feyenoord: 'Mijn rug blokkeerde alles'
items.summary: Jakub Moder keerde donderdag na een half jaar blessureleed terug bij Feyenoord. De 26-jarige middenvelder kan minuten maken in de topper tegen PSV, al komt een basissplaats nog te vroeg.

items.title: Excelsior neemt voortijdig afscheid van huurling Mitrovic
items.summary: De winterse transfermarkt is geopend. In dit blog vind je alle speculaties en bevestigde transfers in Nederland. Bekijk hier ons blog voor de transferperikelen in het buitenland.

items.title: Snoop Dogg aiming to make Swansea a 'global name'
items.summary: Rapper Snoop Dogg says he wants to play his part in making Swansea City become a "global name".

items.title: Lijnders wil bij Man City vruchten plukken van titelrace-ervaring met Liverpool
items.summary: Dankzij herhaaldelijk puntenverlies van koploper Arsenal, doet Manchester City nog volop mee in de strijd om de Premier League-titel. Pepijn Lijnders deed als assistent-manager van Jürgen Klopp genoeg titelrace-ervaring op bij Liverpool en wil daar nu de vruchten van plukken bij The Citizens , vertelde hij tijdens een persmoment voorafgaand aan het duel met Tottenham Hotspur. Volgens de Nederlander is het vooral zaak om de rust te bewaren en zoveel mogelijk wedstrijden te blijven winnen.

items.title: How Fernandez has become key player for Chelsea
items.summary: The stats that show how World Cup winner Enzo Fernandez is one of, if not the, most important players in Chelsea's attack.

items.title: Diarra: 'It was an injustice’ - Inside Senegal’s AFCON final chaos
items.summary: Habib Diarra could only watch on from the sidelines. Helpless, suspended and furious. The Senegal and Sunderland midfielder was observing what he still believes was a sporting injustice unfold in front of him in the final stages of the AFCON final.

items.title: The day English football changed: 10 years on from Manchester City naming Pep Guardiola
items.summary: That 1 February 2016 announcement led to Johan Cruyff’s gospel spreading to all corners of our game – and a bromance with Neil Warnock It wasn’t quite without fanfare but when Manchester City announced, 10 years ago on Sunday, that Pep Guardiola was to be their manager from the next summer, it was a banal, bald press release that brought English football the news that would change it for ever. That was a simpler time, pre-Brexit and Donald Trump’s presidency, and before centre-halves in League Two would split wide for the keeper to pass out from the back to the holding midfielder, dropping in to receive the ball as a false 9 came deep to link with full-backs stepping into midfield. “It’s not about coaches adapting to English football,” said Jordi Cruyff in 2016 as Guardiola began to make his mark on England. “It’s about English football adapting to the new things of the game.” And yet that typical Cruyffian confidence looked like hubris when Guardiola’s Manchester City got hammered 4-2 by Leicester, 4-0 by Everton and experienced Champions League humiliations at Barcelona and Monaco in that first season. Continue reading...

items.title: I never stopped believing - Calvert-Lewin
items.summary: Leeds striker Dominic Calvert-Lewin sits down with BBC Sport's Betty Glover to talk about his return to goalscoring form, being a free agent and his England chances.

items.summary: Florian Wirtz sits down with Kelly Somers to talk about his slow start at Anfield, playing padel and making Bundesliga history.

items.title: Marshman and Diligent Harry lock horns again in Kachy showdown
items.summary: Marshman and Diligent Harry are all set to do battle in this afternoon's feature Kachy Stakes from Lingfield, live on Sky Sports Racing.

items.title: Inter Miami signs Berterame to long-term DP deal
items.summary: Inter Miami signed Mexico national team forward Germán Berterame from Monterrey as a designated player with a contract through the 2028-29 MLS season.

items.title: Madrid handed Benfica, Jose rematch in UCL draw
items.summary: Real Madrid have been handed a rematch with Jose Mourinho's Benfica in the Champions League knockout playoff round.

items.title: Man City to support Dias after home burglary
items.summary: Manchester City assistant coach Pep Lijnders confirmed the club are supporting Rúben Dias after the home he shares with his partner, TV presenter Maya Jama, was burgled.

items.title: Ronaldo scores 17th goal of season for Al Nassr
items.summary: Cristiano Ronaldo on Friday scored his 17th goal for Al Nassr, opening the scoring in the match against Al Kholood at the King Abdullah Sport City Stadium in Buraydah, for a 3-0 Al Nassr win.
